system.concept: The antlered, silver-speared huntsman of the Great Wood, who claims the souls of the dead.
system.description: On certain nights, the Pale Hunter stalks the Great Wood with his ghostly hounds, and even the bravest hunters stay in town. The Forest Folk spoke of him as something like a force of nature — less a god to worship than a storm to endure. Tales differ on what happens should you cross his path: he might ignore you, call you to join him, or mark you as his quarry. But all agree: impress him, and you'll be well rewarded; get caught, or fail to do your part — well, best not to think on it. He appears as a looming, antlered figure, luminous white, his face in shadow, his straight spear tipped with a silver crescent, draped with the jangling trophies of past hunts.

questions.prompt: What is he — spirit, ghost, or something else?
How is he connected to the moon?
What is his relationship with the Lady of Crows?
What hero won his respect, and how? What reward did they earn?

lore.prompt: **Something interesting:** He forgoes all other prey to hunt the undead, pursuing them even outside the Great Wood and on nights he would not normally roam.
**Something useful:** To set the Hunter upon an undead being, acquire an item that bears its spirit-scent, and burn it on a moonlit night in a smoky fire of fresh-cut cedar boughs.
